title: Design the feature freshness architecture for a recommendation system where "user just viewed this exact product 30 seconds ago" needs to influence the next request's ranking.
prompt:
Design the feature freshness architecture for a recommendation system where "user just viewed this exact product 30 seconds ago" needs to influence the next request's ranking.

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
This needs a streaming feature pipeline sitting alongside the existing batch pipeline, not replacing it. Page views and clicks flow into a stream, a stream processor computes short-window features like "items viewed in the last 5 minutes" and writes them to a low-latency online store, and at request time the ranking model pulls both slow-changing batch features and fresh streaming features from the same feature store's online path. A 6-hour refresh cycle can't capture "viewed 30 seconds ago" by definition, so you need a fundamentally different data path with sub-minute latency.

**Takeaway:** Streaming and batch feature definitions need to share a transformation library, or the same logical feature will drift apart between the two paths.
hint_notes: Add a streaming feature pipeline alongside batch, not replacing it — stream processor computes short-window features (last 5 min) into a low-latency online store. Share a transformation library between batch/streaming paths or the same feature drifts apart between them.
